sections.heading: PART II STATEMENT OF POLICY
sections.title: Policy statement for payment service providers' cybersecurity
sections.summary: This section establishes the purpose and scope of cybersecurity requirements for Payment Service Providers (PSPs) in Kenya. It mandates that PSPs develop cybersecurity strategies, frameworks, and policies to mitigate cyber risks while ensuring business continuity and financial stability. The guidelines require PSPs to align cybersecurity practices with their overall risk management and business objectives, with the Central Bank of Kenya (CBK) overseeing compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines the foundational cybersecurity governance framework for PSPs, requiring them to implement risk-based cybersecurity programs that protect critical payment systems. It directly impacts DTIAs by mandating specific risk assessments and incident response protocols for cross-border data transfers involving payment systems.

requirements.text: PSPs must develop cybersecurity strategies, frameworks, and policies to mitigate cyber risks
PSPs must align cybersecurity practices with overall business risk management objectives
PSPs must maintain business continuity capabilities during cyber incidents
PSPs must ensure cybersecurity programs address critical payment system risks

sections.source_quote: The Guidelines outline the minimum requirements that PSPs shall build upon in the development and implementation of strategies, frameworks, policies, procedures and related activities aimed at mitigating cyber risk.
sections.relevance_reason: Core cybersecurity and financial data compliance for PSPs

sections.heading: PART III SPECIFIC REQUIREMENTS
sections.title: Specific cybersecurity governance requirements for payment service providers
sections.summary: This section details specific governance requirements for Payment Service Providers (PSPs) in Kenya, including board responsibilities, senior management roles, and the Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) function. It mandates that PSPs establish clear accountability structures, implement robust cybersecurity programs, and conduct regular risk assessments to ensure the security of payment systems and customer data.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires PSPs to implement comprehensive cybersecurity governance structures that directly impact data transfer assessments by mandating specific risk management protocols for cross-border payment systems and third-party data handling.

requirements.text: Board of directors must oversee cybersecurity strategy development and implementation
Board must review cybersecurity preparedness against cyber risk profile quarterly
Senior management must implement cybersecurity strategy and monitor cyber threats
CISO must report cybersecurity posture to board quarterly
PSPs must maintain a cybersecurity incident response plan
PSPs must conduct post-incident analysis to prevent future breaches

sections.source_quote: The board of directors is ultimately responsible for the cybersecurity of the PSP.
sections.relevance_reason: Core cybersecurity governance for financial data

sections.title: Transitional periods and reporting requirements for cybersecurity
sections.summary: This section outlines transitional periods and reporting requirements for Payment Service Providers (PSPs) in Kenya, including the need to maintain cybersecurity incident records and templates. It mandates that PSPs document and report cybersecurity incidents in specific formats to ensure timely regulatory oversight and compliance with national payment system security standards.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires PSPs to maintain detailed incident records for cybersecurity events, which is critical for DTIAs as it establishes baseline reporting requirements for cross-border data transfers involving payment systems.

requirements.text: PSPs must maintain immediate cybersecurity incident records
PSPs must maintain quarterly cybersecurity incident records

sections.source_quote: Cybersecurity incident record template (Immediate) and Cybersecurity incident record template (Quarterly)
sections.relevance_reason: Incident reporting and breach notification for financial data

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Cybersecurity training, monitoring, and reporting requirements for PSPs
sections.summary: PSPs must conduct regular cybersecurity training for staff and users to improve technical proficiency and cyber hygiene. They must implement processes to monitor IT systems for timely detection of cybersecurity events and incidents. PSPs must report to the CEO quarterly on information system confidentiality, integrity, availability, policy exceptions, cybersecurity program effectiveness, and material cyber events. They must also report to the Board quarterly on cybersecurity management capabilities and strategy implementation progress. Additionally, PSPs must update incident response mechanisms and Business Continuity Plans based on threat intelligence, incorporate scenario analysis for cyber-attacks, ensure critical backups with recovery objectives, define cyber risk roles, implement disaster recovery tests, assess cybersecurity program effectiveness, and report cybersecurity posture to senior management, board, and audit committees.
sections.dtia_summary: PSPs must maintain robust cybersecurity monitoring, reporting, and incident response mechanisms to ensure data protection across borders. These requirements directly impact cross-border data transfers by mandating timely incident reporting and resilience measures for critical systems.

requirements.text: Organize professional cyber-related trainings to improve staff technical proficiency and user awareness for cyber hygiene
Ensure adequate processes for monitoring IT systems to detect cybersecurity events and incidents timely
Report to CEO quarterly on information system confidentiality, integrity, and availability assessments
Report to CEO quarterly on detailed exceptions to approved cybersecurity policies and procedures
Report to CEO quarterly on effectiveness of approved cybersecurity program
Report to CEO quarterly on material cybersecurity events affecting PSP during period
Report to Board quarterly on PSP's capability to manage cybersecurity and progress in implementation of cybersecurity strategy and goals
Ensure timely update of incident response mechanism and Business Continuity Plan based on latest cyber threat intelligence
Incorporate scenario analysis to consider material cyber-attacks, mitigating actions, and identify control gaps
Ensure adequate backups of critical IT systems and data with recovery objectives to sites unlikely affected by disasters
Define, document, and communicate roles and responsibilities for managing cyber risks including emergency decision-making
Implement Business Continuity Plan and disaster recovery test plans to ensure PSP functions post cyber-attack
Assess overall effectiveness of PSP's cybersecurity program
Periodically report on organization's cybersecurity posture to senior management, board, and audit committee

sections.source_quote: e) Organizing professional cyber related trainings to improve technical proficiency of staff and user awareness trainings for improved cyber hygiene. f) Ensure that adequate processes are in place for monitoring IT systems to detect cybersecurity events and incidents in a timely manner. g) Reporting to the CEO on an agreed interval but not less than once per quarter on the following: ... h) Reporting to the Board on an agreed interval but not less than once per quarter on the PSP's capability to manage cybersecurity and progress in implementation of the cybersecurity strategy and goals. i) Ensure timely update of incident response mechanism and Business Continuity Plan (BCP) based on the latest cyber threat intelligence gathered. j) Incorporate the utilization of scenario analysis to consider a material cyber-attack, mitigating actions, and identify potential control gaps. k) Ensure adequate backups of critical IT systems and data in line with predetermined recovery objectives (e.g. real time back up of changes made to critical data) are carried out to a site that is unlikely to be affected by a disaster event at the main processing site. l) Ensure the roles and responsibilities of managing cyber risks, including in emergency or crisis decision-making, are clearly defined, documented and communicated to relevant staff. m) Put in place BCP and disaster recovery test plans to ensure that the PSP can continue to function and meet its regulatory obligations in the event of an unforeseen attack through cyber-crime. n) Assessing the overall effectiveness of the PSP's cybersecurity program. o) Periodically reporting on the organization's cybersecurity posture to senior management, board and audit committee.
sections.relevance_reason: Cybersecurity training, incident reporting, breach notification

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Cyber incident response and resilience requirements for payment systems
sections.summary: Payment service providers must notify CBK or credible sources of malware infections, data breaches, or unauthorized system access. They must develop incident response plans covering internal processes, roles, communications, remediation requirements, documentation, and plan revisions. PSPs must also maintain situational awareness, secure offline storage of financial records, and conduct tests to prevent significant customer service disruptions.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires PSPs to have robust incident response capabilities and resilience planning for cross-border data transfers involving payment systems, ensuring timely breach detection and recovery.

requirements.text: PSPs must report malware infections or unauthorized system access to CBK or credible sources
PSPs must notify CBK of data breaches or potential breaches
PSPs must develop incident response plans covering internal processes, roles, communications, remediation, documentation, and plan revisions
PSPs must maintain situational awareness to predict and respond to environmental changes
PSPs must store critical financial records securely offline for restoration after disruptions
PSPs must conduct tests to prevent significant customer service disruptions from cyber events

sections.source_quote: a) a PSP or its agent is informed, through any source, of the installation or existence of any malware in any of its systems or environments, or any system or environment of one of its agents, no matter where such malware is located or how it was introduced; b) a PSP or its agent receives notification from CBK, or any other credible source that the PSP or its agent(s) has experienced a data breach or a potential data breach; or c) a PSP or its agent discovers or, in the exercise of reasonable diligence, should have discovered a data breach or unauthorized penetration of its own system or environment or the system or environment of its agent(s).
sections.relevance_reason: Cyber incident response, breach notification, financial data handling

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Cybersecurity incident reporting requirements
sections.summary: PSPs must notify CBK within 24 hours and SWIPS/SIPS within 2 hours of significant cybersecurity incidents affecting services, reputation, or financial condition. They must also submit comprehensive incident reports quarterly.
sections.dtia_summary: Mandates immediate reporting of critical incidents to regulators, ensuring timely intervention for data transfers and financial stability.

requirements.text: PSPs must notify CBK within 24 hours of significant cybersecurity incidents impacting services, reputation, or financial condition.
SWIPS and SIPS must notify CBK within 2 hours of significant cybersecurity incidents.
PSPs must submit quarterly cybersecurity incident reports to CBK by the 10th day after each quarter end.

sections.source_quote: PSPs should notify CBK within 24 hours, and SWIPS and SIPS within 2 hours, of any Cybersecurity incident(s) that could have a significant and adverse impact on the PSP's ability to provide adequate services to its customers, its reputation or financial condition in the format set out as Annex II (Immediate) to this Guideline. This should be followed by a comprehensive report on the incident. On a quarterly basis, PSPs shall provide Central Bank of Kenya with a report in the format set out as Annex III (Quarterly) to this Guideline, concerning its occurrence and handling of Cybersecurity incidents.
